title: Kubernetes Container Infrastructure
subtitle: How Kubernetes actually works — Pods, Deployments, Services, networking, and the orchestration patterns that scale
description: A practical course on Kubernetes for engineers and operators. Covers the mental model (the control loop, declarative API, controllers), core objects (Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments, StatefulSets, DaemonSets), networking (Services, Ingress, NetworkPolicies), storage (PV/PVC, StorageClasses), security (RBAC, secrets, security contexts), and operations (observability, autoscaling, upgrades, common failure modes). Built from the official Kubernetes documentation, the CNCF ecosystem, and battle-tested patterns. Not Kubernetes-evangelism; just how it works, when it fits, and how to operate it without losing weekends.
audience: Developers and ops engineers running or planning to run containerized workloads. Container familiarity helps; Kubernetes background not required.
prerequisites: Basic familiarity with containers (you've used Docker)
Comfort with YAML and command-line tools
outcomes: Explain Kubernetes' control loop and declarative API
Use the core objects (Pods, Deployments, Services, StatefulSets) confidently
Design networking with Services, Ingress, and NetworkPolicies
Configure storage with PV/PVC and StorageClasses
Apply RBAC, secrets, and security contexts correctly
Run autoscaling and rolling updates without outages
Diagnose common failure modes (CrashLoopBackOff, ImagePullBackOff, OOMKilled, etc.)
Know when Kubernetes is the right tool and when it isn't
